title: Media Console with DVD Drawer Storage (Cyndi Collection)
summary: A wide plywood media console with two DVD drawers, two door cabinets with in-door storage racks, and a jigsaw-cut footer. A full cabinet build with framed doors and drawer faces.
description:
This is a Pottery Barn–inspired media console: a wide plywood carcass with a central open shelf bay flanked by two door cabinets, plus two drawers sized to hold DVDs behind framed faces. The doors carry in-door storage racks (shallow shelves on the back of each door), and the whole thing sits on a jigsaw-cut decorative footer. It's real casework — face-fitted doors, drawer slides, drawer faces shimmed to even gaps — and squaring the carcass is the make-or-break step.

The cut list is built around three plywood widths the plan calls 1x20, 1x12, and 1x8 (3/4" plywood ripped to 20", 11-1/4", and 7-1/4"). Measure your actual plywood — if your 1x12 boards aren't a true 11-1/4", rip them down, because the door and drawer clearances are cut to those exact widths. The plan is blunt that if you don't square the box carefully, the drawers and doors won't fit.

The doors and drawer faces use the same easy method: a 1/2" plywood center panel framed in 1x3, with the Kreg jig reset between 1/2" stock (panel-to-frame) and 3/4" stock (frame-to-frame). Two fit rules recur: inset the drawer boxes 3/4" from the front to leave room for the faces, and give doors and faces a consistent 1/8" gap all around. The builder's trick for perfect drawer faces — center the face on the closed drawer box, tack it with a couple of 1/4" nails through the front, then open the drawer and screw it solidly from the back. Finish or paint.

tools.note: Ripping the plywood to the three exact widths the plan depends on.

materials.name: Plywood or MDF, 1/2 in, quarter sheet
1x8, 8 ft
materials.note: Door storage boxes and drawer sides.

materials.name: Finish nails, 1-1/4 in and 1/4 in brads

cutList.material: Plywood or MDF, 1/2 in, quarter sheet
Plywood or MDF, 1/2 in, quarter sheet

steps.title: Attach the shelf partitions
steps.body: Attach the vertical partitions with pocket holes set for 3/4" stock and 1-1/4" screws, or 2" countersunk screws, with glue. Take extra care to square the project up here — the drawers and doors won't fit if the box isn't square.

steps.title: Add the top and bottom
steps.body: Add the top and bottom to the partitions with pocket holes. The center drawer divider may be hard to reach with pocket holes due to drill clearance, so fasten it from the bottom of the cabinet instead. Adjust for square.

steps.title: Add the sides
steps.body: The sides are 1x12s and the plan expects a true 11-1/4" width — measure yours and rip down if wider. Attach them with a 1/2" gap on the outsides as shown.

steps.title: Add the cabinet shelves
steps.body: Add a shelf in each side cabinet. Fix them by screwing in place, or drill holes for shelf pins if you want them adjustable.

steps.title: Cut and attach the footer
steps.body: Mark and cut the decorative footer with a jigsaw, then sand the cut edges smooth to match — a circular saw is better for the long straight portions. The front footer is slightly different from the sides but shares the pattern. Attach the side footers to the 2x2s, then the front and back footers to the side footers and cleats. Attach the whole base to the cabinet with 2" screws and glue.

steps.title: Build the in-door storage boxes
steps.body: Build two door-storage boxes from 1x8, very square, checking that your 1x8 stock is a true 7-1/4" wide. Add the optional 1x2 ledges to keep contents from falling out when the door swings.

steps.title: Build and hang the doors
steps.body: Build each door from a 1/2" plywood center panel framed in 1x3 — set the Kreg jig for 1/2" stock to fasten the panel to the frame, then for 3/4" stock to join the frame boards. Attach each door to its storage box, outside edges flush, with pocket holes from inside or finish nails from outside. Hang the doors with narrow-profile hinges, giving 1/8" clearance top and bottom, and adjust the hinges until each closes smoothly.
materials: Plywood or MDF, 1/2 in, quarter sheet
Finish nails, 1-1/4 in and 1/4 in brads

steps.title: Build the drawers and install the slides
steps.body: Build the two drawer boxes from 1x8, nail the plywood bottoms on with glue and 1-1/4" nails, and keep them very square. Install the 16" slides, remembering to inset the drawer box 3/4" from the front to leave room for the face.
materials: Finish nails, 1-1/4 in and 1/4 in brads

steps.title: Fit the drawer faces and finish
steps.body: Build the drawer faces like the doors (1/2" panel framed in 1x3), sized for a 1/8" gap all around. With the drawer box fully inserted, center the face over the opening, tack it with a few 1/4" nails through the face frame, then open the drawer and screw the face on solidly from the backside — mind where the pulls will go. Add pulls, fill holes, sand, and finish or paint.
materials: Plywood or MDF, 1/2 in, quarter sheet
Finish nails, 1-1/4 in and 1/4 in brads

images.alt: Wide media console with DVD drawers and two door cabinets
